chapter_title.ar: باب اللِّبَاسِ الَّذِي تُكْرَهُ الصَّلَاةُ فِيهِ وَ مَا لَا تُكْرَهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 60 - The clothes in which the Salāt is disliked, and what is not disliked

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/320)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/320)
text: الْخَزِّ الْخَالِصِ أَنَّهُ لَا بَأْسَ بِهِ فَأَمَّا الَّذِي يُخْلَطُ فِيهِ وَبَرُ الْأَرَانِبِ أَوْ غَيْرُ ذَلِكَ مِمَّا يُشْبِهُ هَذَا فَلَا تُصَلِّ فِيهِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, raising it,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws regarding the pure otter (skin) that there is no problem with it, but as for that wherein is mixed the fur of rabbits or other than that from what resembles it, so you cannot pray Salāt in it’.
en.sarwar: 26. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad who, in a marfu‘ manner has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about pure al-Khazz has said, ‘It is not an offense to perform Salah (prayer) with it, but do not perform Salah (prayer) with that which is mixed with fur of foxes or other things.’”
